title: Set up PocketBase as a self-hosted backend for Next.js
summary: Run PocketBase as a standalone backend, configure collections and auth, integrate the JS SDK with Next.js, protect routes with PocketBase sessions, and deploy PocketBase alongside your app.
declaration: I will integrate PocketBase — a self-hosted open-source backend (Go binary) — with this Next.js app. PocketBase provides a SQLite database, built-in auth, real-time subscriptions, file storage, and an admin UI, all in a single executable. If you prefer Supabase or Firebase instead, say so before I begin.
